\doxysubsection{Detailed Description}
Numerical engine for Sommerfeld integrals in stratified media. 

Construct with material stacks and numerical knobs (semi-\/ellipse radii, ratio, cutoff), then use \doxylink{classSommerfeldIntegrator_a3f148b96c090e06e67fb00aea41e7f94}{Evaluate()} to get intra-\//inter-\/layer Green coefficients packed for interpolation. 

Definition at line \mbox{\hyperlink{SommerfeldIntegrator_8h_source_l00036}{36}} of file \mbox{\hyperlink{SommerfeldIntegrator_8h_source}{Sommerfeld\+Integrator.\+h}}.

Constructs a \doxylink{classSommerfeldIntegrator}{Sommerfeld\+Integrator} object for layered media calculations. 

This constructor initializes the Sommerfeld integrator with the given parameters, which define the properties of the layered medium and numerical settings for integration.


\begin{DoxyParams}{Parameters}
{\em k\+\_\+L} & A vector of complex wave numbers for the layered medium. \\
\hline
{\em in\+Material\+Index} & An integer indicating the observation/source intra-\/layer index for calculations. \\
\hline
{\em in\+Epsilon\+Medium} & A vector of complex permittivity values for the medium layers. \\
\hline
{\em in\+Mu\+Medium} & A vector of complex permeability values for the medium layers. \\
\hline
{\em in\+ZVals\+Interfaces} & A vector of interface positions along the z-\/axis. \\
\hline
{\em in\+Thickness} & A vector of layer thicknesses. \\
\hline
{\em in\+Semi\+Real} & Scaling factor for real axis of semiellipse. \\
\hline
{\em in\+Semi\+Imag} & Scaling factor for imaginary axis of semiellipse. \\
\hline
{\em in\+Ratio} & A double defining the integration ratio parameter. \\
\hline
{\em in\+Cutoff} & A double specifying the cutoff value for the integration. \\
\hline
\end{DoxyParams}

Computes the Fresnel reflection and transmission coefficients between two layers.

Computes the inter-\/layer integration function. 

This function evaluates the integral of the inter-\/layer Green\textquotesingle{}s function over a specified path. The integration can be performed along a semi-\/ellipse, real infinity, or imaginary infinity.


\begin{DoxyParams}{Parameters}
{\em tq\+\_\+te} & Quasistatic TE transmission coefficient. \\
\hline
{\em tq\+\_\+tm} & Quasistatic TM transmission coefficient. \\
\hline

\hline
{\em x} & Double representing the integration parameter. \\
\hline
{\em k\+\_\+max} & Parameter for integration path specification. \\
\hline
{\em int\+Zero\+Krho} & An \doxylink{structIntegrandInter}{Integrand\+Inter} structure containing precomputed singularity corrections. \\
\hline
{\em path} & A string specifying the integration path (\textquotesingle{}semi\textquotesingle{}, \textquotesingle{}real\textquotesingle{}, or \textquotesingle{}imag\textquotesingle{}).\\
\hline
\end{DoxyParams}
\begin{DoxyReturn}{Returns}
An \doxylink{structIntegrandInter}{Integrand\+Inter} structure containing the integrated Green function elements.
\end{DoxyReturn}

\begin{DoxyExceptions}{Exceptions}
{\em std\+::invalid\+\_\+argument} & if an invalid path type is specified. \\
\hline
\end{DoxyExceptions}

Computes the inter-\/layer Green\textquotesingle{}s function integrand. 

This function evaluates the inter-\/layer integrand for a given wavenumber, using radial and axial coordinates. It computes Green function elements, derivatives, and surface integrals based on mode selection and operations.


\begin{DoxyParams}{Parameters}
{\em tq\+\_\+te} & Quasistatic TE transmission coefficient. \\
\hline
{\em tq\+\_\+tm} & Quasistatic TM transmission coefficient. \\
\hline
{\em in\+Data3D} & \doxylink{structData3D}{Data3D} structure containing spatial coordinate information. \\
\hline
{\em kr} & Complex radial wavenumber. \\
\hline
{\em mode} & String specifying the mode of operation (\textquotesingle{}bessel\textquotesingle{} or \textquotesingle{}hankel\textquotesingle{}).\\
\hline
\end{DoxyParams}
\begin{DoxyReturn}{Returns}
An \doxylink{structIntegrandInter}{Integrand\+Inter} structure containing computed Green function elements and derivatives.
\end{DoxyReturn}

\begin{DoxyExceptions}{Exceptions}
{\em std\+::runtime\+\_\+error} & if input data dimensions mismatch or invalid mode/op is provided.\\
\hline
\end{DoxyExceptions}

Computes secondary wave contributions in a layered medium. 

This function calculates secondary wave fields based on given wavenumber, radial and axial coordinates, and the type of waves involved.


\begin{DoxyParams}{Parameters}
{\em krho} & A complex wavenumber in the radial direction. \\
\hline
{\em r} & A 3D vector containing the radial coordinate values. \\
\hline
{\em z1} & A 3D vector containing the first axial coordinate values. \\
\hline
{\em z2} & A 3D vector containing the second axial coordinate values. \\
\hline
{\em waves} & A string specifying the type of waves considered (e.\+g., TE, TM).\\
\hline
\end{DoxyParams}
\begin{DoxyReturn}{Returns}
A 4D vector containing\+:
\begin{DoxyItemize}
\item f\+: The computed field values.
\item f\+Z1\+: The first Z1-\/derivative of the field.
\item f\+Z2\+: The first Z2-\/derivative of the field.
\item f\+ZZ\+: The mixed Z1-\/\+Z2-\/derivative of the field. 
\end{DoxyItemize}
\end{DoxyReturn}

{\footnotesize\ttfamily \label{classSommerfeldIntegrator_abc3fad85f82353d54bba4336caf1af38} 
\mbox{\hyperlink{structData2D}{Data2D}} Sommerfeld\+Integrator\+::select\+Data2D (\begin{DoxyParamCaption}\item[{const std\+::vector$<$ std\+::vector$<$ double $>$ $>$ \&}]{r}{, }\item[{const std\+::vector$<$ std\+::vector$<$ double $>$ $>$ \&}]{z1}{, }\item[{const std\+::vector$<$ std\+::vector$<$ double $>$ $>$ \&}]{z2}{, }\item[{const std\+::string \&}]{path}{}\end{DoxyParamCaption})}



Selects and processes 2D data based on the given input parameters. 

This function categorizes and stores 2D data depending on the provided path mode (\textquotesingle{}semi\textquotesingle{}, \textquotesingle{}real\textquotesingle{}, or \textquotesingle{}imag\textquotesingle{}). It organizes radial and axial coordinates into matrix or vector formats as needed.


\begin{DoxyParams}{Parameters}
{\em r} & A 2D vector containing the radial coordinate values. \\
\hline
{\em z1} & A 2D vector containing the first axial coordinate values. \\
\hline
{\em z2} & A 2D vector containing the second axial coordinate values (optional). \\
\hline
{\em path} & A string specifying the mode of data selection (\textquotesingle{}semi\textquotesingle{}, \textquotesingle{}real\textquotesingle{}, or \textquotesingle{}imag\textquotesingle{}).\\
\hline
\end{DoxyParams}
\begin{DoxyReturn}{Returns}
A \doxylink{structData2D}{Data2D} structure containing processed coordinate values and indexing information.
\end{DoxyReturn}

\begin{DoxyExceptions}{Exceptions}
{\em std\+::invalid\+\_\+argument} & if an invalid path is specified.\\
\hline
\end{DoxyExceptions}
Pack 2D r/z data depending on path.
\begin{DoxyItemize}
\item "{}semi"{}\+: keep matrices; dense traversal by linear index.
\item "{}real"{}\+: select (r,z) with z \texorpdfstring{$>$}{>}= ratio\texorpdfstring{$\ast$}{*}r (or z1+z2 \texorpdfstring{$>$}{>}= ratio\texorpdfstring{$\ast$}{*}r if provided).
\item "{}imag"{}\+: select (r,z) with z \texorpdfstring{$<$}{<} ratio\texorpdfstring{$\ast$}{*}r (or z1+z2 \texorpdfstring{$<$}{<} ratio\texorpdfstring{$\ast$}{*}r if provided). 
\end{DoxyItemize}

Selects and processes 3D data based on the given input parameters. 

This function categorizes and stores 3D data depending on the provided path mode (\textquotesingle{}semi\textquotesingle{}, \textquotesingle{}real\textquotesingle{}, or \textquotesingle{}imag\textquotesingle{}). It organizes radial and axial coordinates into matrix or vector formats as needed.

\begin{DoxyExceptions}{Exceptions}
{\em std\+::invalid\+\_\+argument} & if an invalid path is specified.\\
\hline
\end{DoxyExceptions}
Pack 3D r/z1/z2 data depending on path (semi/real/imag). The real/imag split uses \texorpdfstring{$\vert$}{|}z1 -\/ z2\texorpdfstring{$\vert$}{|} vs ratio\texorpdfstring{$\ast$}{*}r.
